title: 🎙️ Cariboo Local Affairs
description: Today is all about the Cariboo-Chilcotin (chil-KOH-tin) region: the decisions, events, and conversations happening in Williams Lake, Quesnel, 100 Mile House, Bella Coola, and the surrounding region. We're covering local government, community life, economic stories, and the digital equity challenges that shape what it means to live and work in rural BC. - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Today is all about the Cariboo-Chilcotin (chil-KOH-tin) region: the decisions, events, and conversations happening in Williams Lake, Quesnel, 100 Mile House, Bella Coola, and the surrounding region. We're covering local government, community life, economic stories, and the digital equity challenges that shape what it means to live and work in rural BC.
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Cariboo Voices & Local News' — stories FROM and ABOUT the Cariboo-Chilcotin region and its communities. Score 80-100 for articles directly covering: news and events in Williams Lake, Quesnel, 100 Mile House, Bella Coola, Horsefly, Likely, Anahim Lake, Alexis Creek, or the Cariboo Regional District; local government and civic decisions (City of Williams Lake, City of Quesnel, CRD, District of 100 Mile House); local health services and emergency closures; local economy, businesses, and community programs; Cariboo arts, culture, and community events; AND digital equity, rural internet access (fiber, fixed wireless, Starlink), and connectivity serving these specific communities. Articles from local Cariboo news sources (mycariboonow.com, wltribune.com, quesnelobserver.com, 100milefreepress.net, cfjctoday.com) covering Cariboo community life should score 65-85. Score 50-79 for: BC provincial news directly affecting Cariboo communities, rural Canadian community stories closely paralleling Cariboo life, digital equity and rural internet broadly. Score 20-49 for: general BC news that may affect rural areas, broader rural Canada topics without specific Cariboo tie-in. Score 0-19 for: national/international news without Cariboo relevance, pure tech or entertainment with no community angle. Penalise heavily (score 0–20): international geopolitical and war news with no Cariboo tie-in; science discoveries without a local community connection; consumer tech reviews and gadget guides; wildlife and conservation stories not involving Cariboo communities or First Nations; BC provincial news without direct Cariboo-region relevance. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [Boing Boing] An AWS bug turned a 5-cent bill into $2.5 billion
items.content_html:
🏔️ ✏️

On the morning of July 17, a redditor who normally pays Amazon Web Services about five cents a month opened their inbox to a wall of billing alerts and a running tab of $2.5 billion. Their whole setup was a single S3 storage bucket, untouched since 2023 and not even public. — Read the rest

The post An AWS bug turned a 5-cent bill into $2.5 billion appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [ScienceDaily] A tiny universe in a bottle reveals clues to the origins of life
items.content_html:
🏔️ ✏️

Researchers have created cosmic dust from scratch by recreating space-like conditions inside glass tubes. The dust contains complex carbon-rich molecules built from elements essential to life and produces infrared signals similar to real material found in space. By studying these laboratory samples, scientists can explore how organic chemistry unfolds around stars and how comets, asteroids and meteorites may have carried those ingredients to Earth.

items.title: [Boing Boing] A whole language built from about 120 words
items.content_html:
🏔️ ✏️

Most invented languages try to say everything. Toki Pona tries to say just enough — with about 120 words and 14 sounds. The Canadian translator Sonja Lang built it in 2001, while she was struggling with depression, as a way to simplify her own thoughts. — Read the rest

items.title: [Williams Lake Tribune] Death during annual swim event confirmed as 5th drowning on Okanagan this year
items.content_html:
🏔️ ✏️

## Death during annual swim event confirmed as 5th drowning on Okanagan this year Published 8:45 pm Sunday, July 19, 2026 **By Ty Lim** A fire truck parks metres away from Tugboat Beach in Kelowna while crews respond to an emergency. (Brady Ursel) A person who drowned at Tugboat Bay was participating in the annual Across the Lake Swim, Kelowna RCMP have confirmed. items.title: [ScienceDaily] New AI blood test predicts heart disease 15 years early
items.content_html:
🏔️ ✏️

A new AI-powered blood test could give people a remarkably early warning of serious heart and circulation problems. Developed by researchers at the University of Hong Kong, CardiOmicScore analyzes thousands of proteins and metabolites to estimate the risk of six major cardiovascular diseases, including heart attack, stroke, heart failure, and atrial fibrillation. Unlike genetic risk scores, which remain fixed throughout life, the system captures biological changes linked to a person’s current health, lifestyle, and environment.

items.title: [The Marginalian] How to Bear Your Sorrows: Nick Cave on Integrating the Darkness of Loss with the Bright Ongoingness of Life
items.content_html:
🏔️ ✏️

Few things in our culture are more wounding than the concept of healing — as if the pains and losses that we suffer are an illness, a malfunction of the psyche to be cut out like a tumor, rather than a natural function of being alive, of feeling life deeply and living it fully. All our sorrows have been wasted if we have not learned to suffer and to be surprised by the door at the end of our suffering, on the other wide of which is simply the world, which keeps on worlding. It is not healing that allows… read article

items.title: [ScienceDaily] Scientists discover why peach fuzz can suddenly make you itch
items.content_html:
🏔️ ✏️

A newly discovered network of fine hairs and specialized nerve cells appears to form a dedicated system for sensing mechanical itch, offering fresh insight into why chronic itching occurs. Because humans show signs of having the same pathway, the research could pave the way for more effective treatments for conditions such as eczema.

items.title: EBSCO Information Services Launches Filipino Studies Source to Advance Philippine Studies Research
items.content_html:
🏔️ ✏️

EBSCO Information Services Launches Filipino Studies Source to Advance Philippine Studies Research kdonnelly@ebsco.com Wed, 07/15/2026 - 13:47 ~ New full-text database brings together journals, e-books and locally published research for Filipino studies ~
Product line
Research Databases
Market
Academic Libraries
Publish date
Thu, 07/16/2026 - 10:00

IPSWICH, Mass. — July 16, 2026 — EBSCO Information Services (EBSCO) announces the release of Filipino Studies Source, a new full-text database designed to support teaching, learning and research focused on the Philippines. Combining journals, magazines and e-books from respected academic publishers and Philippine institutions, the database offers researchers a trusted resource for exploring the country's rich history, culture, society and contemporary issues.

Developed to meet the growing demand for authoritative regional scholarship, Filipino Studies Source provides access to 116 active full-text peer-reviewed journals and magazines together with 50 e-book titles. The collection features publications from leading Philippine universities, professional associations and research institutions, making locally produced scholarship more discoverable for researchers around the world.

The database supports interdisciplinary research across a broad range of academic fields, including business, economics, education, environmental studies, health, humanities, law, literature, medicine, science and technology. Researchers can explore topics spanning Filipino language and literature, politics, religion, sociology, biodiversity, education and cultural studies through high-quality content with strong local and regional relevance.

EBSCO Information Services Senior Vice President of Content Sara Earley says Filipino Studies Source reflects EBSCO's commitment to expanding access to regionally significant content. "Filipino Studies Source brings together essential scholarly resources that showcase the breadth and depth of research on the Philippines. By increasing access to locally published, peer-reviewed scholarship alongside international research, the database helps students, faculty and researchers explore Filipino studies through a more comprehensive and balanced collection of perspectives." 

Available on the familiar EBSCOhost platform, Filipino Studies Source also incorporates EBSCO's AI-supported research experience while benefiting from an intuitive interface that makes discovering and navigating scholarly content more efficient for users at every academic level.

For more information, visit Filipino Studies Source.

About EBSCO Information Services
EBSCO Information Services (EBSCO) is a leading provider of online research content and search technologies via the world-renowned EBSCOhost® research service, serving universities & colleges; K12 schools & public libraries; healthcare and medical institutions; corporations; and government agencies. Providing research, discovery, acquisition management & subscription services, and clinical decision support, EBSCO provides institutions with access to content and technology to serve the information and workflow needs of their users and organizations. For more information, visit the EBSCO website. Visit our blog at EBSCOpost or follow us on X, Facebook, LinkedIn and Instagram.

 ###

For more information, please contact:

Nawat Tangpinijkarn
Product Marketing Manager
EBSCO Information Services
nawat@ebsco.com

EBSCO Information Services Launches Filipino Studies Source to Advance Philippine Studies Research

items.title: [The Marginalian] Borges on How to Conquer Time
items.content_html:
🏔️ ✏️

"Time is the substance I am made of. Time is a river which sweeps me along, but I am the river; it is a tiger which destroys me, but I am the tiger; it is a fire which consumes me, but I am the fire."
items.summary: "Time is the substance I am made of. Time is a river which sweeps me along, but I am the river; it is a tiger which destroys me, but I am the tiger; it is a fire which consumes me, but I am the fire."
